published.text:
### Existing hero + proof framing retained
Section: Hero and proof-library positioning
- Keeps the opening focused on three documented proof assets:
  - Paris Cafe voice agent
  - e-commerce CRM automation
  - Instagram lead-generation pipeline
- Keeps direct contextual links into:
  - /best-ai-automation-for-service-businesses
  - /guides
  - /ai-automation-consultant-pricing-small-business
  - /ai-automation-cost-small-business
  - /what-does-an-ai-automation-consultant-do
- Purpose stays the same: make `/case-studies` a proof layer, not a generic portfolio page.

### New fit / not-fit routing block
Section: Good fit for this hub / Better starting points if not
- Adds a new answer-extractable top-of-hub routing layer with two explicit paths:
  - Start with these case studies if you need proof before you decide
  - Skip this hub first if your real question is workflow choice, fit, or pricing
- New routing links point directly into:
  - /best-ai-automation-for-service-businesses
  - /ai-automation-consultant-small-business
  - /guides
  - /ai-automation-consultant-pricing-small-business
- Purpose: clarify the role of `/case-studies` for both buyers and crawlers, while strengthening internal-link context into adjacent decision/support pages.

### FAQ expansion
Section: Buyer questions this proof hub answers
- Adds a new question:
  - “Should I read the case studies or the guides hub first?”
- Answer frames the preferred reading order as:
  - proof first when trust is the blocker
  - guides first when workflow choice is already the main question
  - consultant/pricing pages next when expert help is on the table
- Keeps the tone practical and navigation-focused instead of generic.

### Existing proof-routing retained
Section: Which case study should you read first? / Connect proof to the priority pages
- Keeps the existing proof-to-page mapping intact:
  - Paris Cafe → booking / phone / appointment-setter paths
  - e-commerce CRM → consultant / lead-follow-up / guides
  - Instagram lead-generation → consultant / best-first-automation / tool-comparison support
- The new fit/not-fit section makes those routes easier to interpret before a reader scrolls deeper into the page.

edits.description: This was a bounded indexing-support refresh on an existing discovered-not-indexed hub page, not a new page ship.
The new copy is explicit about when this page is the right starting point and when another page is better. That is useful for both human readers and answer engines.
Tone stays practical and anti-fluff: proof first, workflow selection second, consultant/pricing only when that is the real question.
Internal links were chosen to reinforce the live proof / guides / consultant / pricing cluster without turning the case-studies hub into a generic link dump.
